blockingIssues.description: `warcraft_status` still returns early for blocked features via `toolError(...)`, so it never exposes the blocked-task workspace/dependency payload that `warcraft_doctor` now reports. The added tests only compare mocked task summaries and do not cover the real blocked-feature early-return path, leaving status/doctor parity for genuine blocked features unproven.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that repo-wide lint is the default finishing gate, but workers may document and escalate unrelated pre-existing `.factory` formatting failures they are forbidden to touch instead of treating them as in-scope fixes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review for `fresh-eyes-attempt-idempotency-fix` cites worker session `5dd53232-4b7b-495a-8173-9e70c2cd9d41`, where `bun run lint` failed only on unrelated `.factory/validation/execution-state-correctness-followup/scrutiny/synthesis.json` formatting while mission guidance forbade editing unrelated dirty files.

rejectedObservations.observation: The repo-level services manifest path was inconsistent with reality because review setup first tried `/Users/beowulf/.factory/services.yaml` before using `/Users/beowulf/Work/personal/opencode-warcraft/.factory/services.yaml`.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected as worker/setup confusion rather than a repo shared-state defect; mission bootstrap already pointed to the repo-root `.factory/services.yaml`, which exists and was used successfully in this scrutiny run.

rejectedObservations.observation: `.gitignore` excludes `.factory/`, so required mission artifacts under `.factory/library/` may still need `git add -f`.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented in the reviewed feature handoff and aligned with current mission expectations; no additional library or services update needed from scrutiny.
